label: Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED, SPLIT WINGS — public LDK in the center, standard pair + common bath in the west wing (with hall node), master + ensuite in the east wing. Maximum master separation; all wet rooms on one contiguous rear run. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

adjacencies.note: east wing stacks master (front) over ensuite (rear) — interior door on master's rear wall
ensuite shares the kitchen's east wall — joins the contiguous rear wet run
common shares the kitchen's west wall — kitchen sits between the two baths: one continuous rear plumbing bay
master doors west off the great_room — the split-wings trade-off: no hall reaches the east wing. Acceptable per PH bungalow practice; add a vestibule on generous shells
br2 (front-west) doors south into the wing hall node
br3 (rear-west) doors north into the wing hall node
the standard pair stacks front-to-rear in the west wing — wall only, no door
common bath doors west into the wing hall node — guests reach it through the hall, not through a bedroom
the wing hall's east face is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
great_room (front-center, hosts entry) and kitchen (rear-center) stack open-plan (gr style)

soft_proximities.note: keep the shared bath on the rear side of the wing hall, next to br3
on generous shells, stretch the hall mouth south so master's door can migrate off the great_room into the hall

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_split_wings_baths_ds_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Three columns across the frontage (west wing ~3.2 m + LDK ~5.0 m + east wing ~3.4 m) make this the widest-hungry parti of the family — the profile triggers at 110 m² and frontage < ~12.0 m should fall back to center_spine before this profile even finishes relaxing.

notes: 3BR wide catalog topology #4 — SPLIT WINGS (public center). Natural floor
band ~92-106 m²; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m at the tight end, happiest
at 13.0-13.5 x 8.0-8.5 m; needs frontage >= ~12.0 m or falls back to
center_spine.
Layout intent (canonical, west = left):
Rear:  [br3   | common | kitchen | ensuite ]
Mid:   [ HALL node    | (great cont.)     ]
Front: [br2   | great_room (entry) | master ]
Program: the public LDK holds the center of the shell — great_room fronts
the street and hosts entry, kitchen behind it. Private zone splits into two
wings: WEST wing carries the standard pair stacked front-to-rear with a
small hall node between them (br2, br3 and the common bath all door onto
it); EAST wing carries master + ensuite. Master doors off the great_room's
east side (bedroom_to_public) — the one privacy concession, softened on
generous shells by migrating the door into a stretched hall mouth.
Separation: master shares no wall with either standard — the two bedroom
wings are a full LDK apart. Best parti for multi-generation households,
rentable-room setups, or parents-vs-teens acoustic separation.
Plumbing: the signature move — kitchen sits BETWEEN the baths (common on its
west wall, ensuite on its east wall), so all three wet rooms plus the
rear-setback dirty kitchen form one contiguous rear plumbing bay. Cheapest
wet infrastructure of the wide family despite 'ds' bath placement.
Corner lots: with bedrooms on both gables and public in the middle, either
gable can face the side street; orient the great_room's front to the main
frontage. Ventilation: every habitable room holds an exterior wall.
Carport: NCP base form; declare via brief on either flank. Mirror when
brief.carport_side='left'.
